old_behavior: Historical robustness operated on the source temperature grid after admissibility filtering.
canonical_requirement: Base temperature candidates must come from the selected TRUSTED_CANONICAL Switching run table (switching_canonical_S_long.csv).
rationale: Preserves grid-based intent while binding to canonical run trust instead of legacy run IDs.

old_behavior: Boundary audit identified 4 K and 30 K as boundary-effect temperatures and recommended exclusion from model-facing interpretation.
canonical_requirement: Exclude T_K=4 and T_K=30 from acceptance metrics; keep them as diagnostic-only boundary checks.
rationale: Directly preserves documented boundary-effect rule from Switching evidence artifacts.

old_behavior: Filtered-collapse logic used forcedRemoveTemps_K=34 and excluded high-T rows when width was missing with peak-at-lowest-current or low-amplitude conditions.
canonical_requirement: Apply explicit high-T admissibility rule before acceptance: forced remove 34 K; for T_K>=30 exclude rows with width_missing and (peak_at_lowest_current OR low_amplitude).
rationale: This is the explicit pre-canonical physics guard already encoded in Switching robustness evidence.

old_behavior: Historical outputs reported temperature-region sensitivity (mid_T and low_T failures) but did not impose a universal hard transition cutoff.
canonical_requirement: Require stratified reporting for the 22-24 K transition band; no automatic exclusion unless boundary/admissibility rules fail.
rationale: Retains scientific caution near transition while avoiding unsupported hard cuts.

old_behavior: Stage1 canonical methods table included only canonical-equivalent perturbations.
canonical_requirement: Allowed perturbations for acceptance are restricted to canonical-equivalent methods: I_peak {max_sample,max_parabolic_local}, width {fwhm_linear,fwhm_nearest,fwhm_fine_interp}, S_peak {max_sample,max_parabolic_local}.
rationale: Preserves the original robustness intent: test estimator sensitivity without changing observable class.

old_behavior: Stage1 explicitly excluded non-equivalent definitions and variant-specific x scaling.
rationale: Maintains canonical-equivalence boundary between robustness and redefinition of observables.

canonical_requirement: Canonical robustness input must be selected from tables/switching_run_trust_classification.csv with classification=TRUSTED_CANONICAL (e.g., run_2026_04_03_000147_switching_canonical).
rationale: Resolves input mismatch while staying inside current canonical system.

old_behavior: Historical evidence was spread across reports/tables_old and missing legacy run folders.
canonical_requirement: Execution inputs must not be loaded from tables_old, root-level tables, or non-trusted legacy runs.
rationale: Separates historical evidence (documentation) from canonical execution inputs.

old_behavior: Pre-canonical Stage1 used fixed canonical collapse coordinate and fixed normalization across variants.
canonical_requirement: Primary acceptance must be invariant in canonical function-space model (no coordinate-collapse perturbation). If auxiliary collapse diagnostics are reported, coordinate/normalization must be fixed and not swept.
rationale: Aligns with docs/switching_canonical_definition.md while preserving comparability intent from Stage1.

old_behavior: Stage1/Stage1B used variant-vs-canonical corr/rmse/relative-deviation plus forensic diagnostics.
canonical_requirement: Mandatory metrics for canonical robustness report: corr_vs_canonical, rmse_abs, median_rel_dev, worst_rel_dev, and Stage1B forensic diagnostics (grid/undersampling and kappa sensitivity decomposition).
rationale: Carries forward scientific meaning of old conclusions into canonical reporting.

old_behavior: Historical verdicts were produced from Stage1 and Stage1B tables/reports.
canonical_requirement: Acceptance requires all: canonical input confirmed, execution signaling valid, and canonical thresholds satisfied on allowed perturbations (I_peak>=0.90, width>=0.85, S_peak>=0.90, kappa1>=0.80, collapse ratio in [0.67,1.50]).
rationale: Combines existing threshold policy with restored canonical-equivalent perturbation scope.

old_behavior: Measurement robustness artifacts used Pearson/Spearman/NRMSE/trend checks across measurement definitions.
canonical_requirement: Treat measurement-definition robustness as a separate contract from parameter robustness acceptance.
rationale: Prevents conflating measurement-family changes with canonical parameter robustness gating.

old_behavior: Recent failure showed wrapper execution did not enter target script.
canonical_requirement: Rerun allowed only if wrapper executes requested script path and execution signaling artifacts are generated from that script run.
rationale: Execution validity is a hard prerequisite for any scientific robustness claim.

old_behavior: Recent run failed required output artifact contract.
canonical_requirement: Rerun allowed only when required robustness tables/report/status fields are produced with canonical naming contract.
rationale: Prevents false-positive completion without contract artifacts.
